title: GeoJSON 3D Terrain Overlay
prompt:
Use Three.js to implement a procedural noise heightmap terrain with hardcoded GeoJSON polygon regions overlaid on top.

[Three.js Import (must follow exactly)]
Place the following importmap in <head> and import via ES Module in <script type="module">:
<script type="importmap">
{ "imports": {
    "three": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/build/three.module.js",
    "three/addons/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/examples/jsm/"
} }
</script>
<script type="module">
import * as THREE from 'three';
import { OrbitControls } from 'three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js';
</script>

[Terrain]
(1) Procedurally generate a 128x128 heightmap (Perlin/Simplex-style noise), rendered as a lit Mesh.
(2) Elevation-based coloring material (gradient from low to high elevation).

[GeoJSON]
(3) Hardcode a simple FeatureCollection with exactly 5 Polygon regions (representing simplified country/administrative boundaries), with coordinates mapped to the terrain plane extent.
(4) Project polygons onto the terrain surface (sample elevation along normals), rendered as semi-transparent colored overlay layers.
(5) Click on a region: highlight that region and display statistics (approximate area, average elevation, etc.) in a DOM panel with id="regionStats".

[Layer Toggles]
(6) id="terrainToggle": toggle terrain Mesh visibility.
(7) id="regionToggle": toggle region overlay layer visibility.

[State Exposure (required)]
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  terrainVertices: number,
  regionCount: number,
  selectedRegion: null | number | string,
  maxElevation: number,
  minElevation: number,
  layerVisibility: { terrain: boolean, regions: boolean }
}

Output a single HTML file that runs directly in the browser.
physics_constraints: Exactly 5 polygon regions from hardcoded GeoJSON
